BA TBA Final exam schedule available to faculty 11/8 Sunday No classes scheduled 11/25 Wednesday No classes scheduled 11/26 – 11/27 Thursday - Friday College closed 11/28 Saturday No classes scheduled 12/14 Monday Last day of classes before Finals Grade rosters available to faculty 12/15 – 12/21 Tuesday – Monday Final Examinations Fall 2026 semester ends 12/24 – 12/25 Thursday – Friday College is closed 12/28 Monday Deadline to submit grades in CUNYfirst 12/31 Thursday College is closed Explanation of Terms ePermit and Permit — If you are interested in taking a course at another CUNY campus or another college, you must apply for an ePermit or Permit to do so. Evening Classes — Evening classes begin at 4:35 pm or later. To be notified when the College is closed due to severe weather, you should register for CUNYAlert

pm or later. To be notified when the College is closed due to severe weather, you should register for CUNYAlert . Financial Aid Enrollment Status Certification date — your enrollment status on this date is used to determine your financial aid. VOE — Verification of Enrollment. Faculty must verify your enrollment in their class in order to determine WN grades (see below). W Grade – You may withdraw from classes after the last day to drop for a 25% tuition refund, through the last day to withdraw. You will receive a “W” grade that is not calculated into your GPA. Students who officially withdraw from a course at this point are NOT eligible for a tuition refund. Wait List — If a class you want to take is filled, you can join a wait list on CUNYfirst. Learn about waitlisting. WD grade — A Withdraw/Drop grade indicates that you attended a class at least once before dropping it. You will receive a “WD” grade if you drop a course after

you attended a class at least once before dropping it. You will receive a “WD” grade if you drop a course after the Financial Aid Enrollment Status Certification date , until the last day to drop a course for 25% Tuition Refund. A “WD” grade does not appear on your official transcript and is not calculated into your GPA. WN grade — A Withdrawn/Never Attended grade will be assigned to you if you are certified as having never attended the class by the faculty, on the Verification of Enrollment Roster. A “WN” grade does not appear on your official transcript and is not calculated into your GPA. Note: Students are protected by New York State law and cannot be penalized for missing any scheduled academic activities that may conflict with their religious observances. For more info, please visit CUNY’s Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Hub and CUNY’s Holiday Calendar . Arrow Up To Top
and CUNY’s Holiday Calendar . Arrow Up To Top

Financial Aid | Hunter College Financial Aid A college degree maximizes your chances of being successful, but knowing how to invest in your education can be challenging. Use the information below to help you get started and learn more about your financial aid options. Important Deadlines Workshops & Events Financial Aid Verification Financial Aid Calculators Frequently Asked Questions Create a Support Ticket Fraudulent emails about financial aid refunds are circulating. Do not click any links. Delete the message. If you clicked a link or shared information, contact StudentHelpdesk@hunter.cuny.edu immediately. FAFSA/TAP Workshops FAFSA/TAP workshops are cancelled through June. Please check back for updates. If you have questions about filing a FAFSA/TAP application, visit us in Room 241, North Building. CUNYfirst Financial Aid Guide Learn how to access, view, and manage your financial aid on CUNYfirst. Download the guide 2026-27 FAFSA Now Available Hunter's priority deadline

financial aid on CUNYfirst. Download the guide 2026-27 FAFSA Now Available Hunter's priority deadline for filing your FAFSA application is April 1 , but don't wait until then. By filing your FAFSA sooner, you will find out about your eligibility sooner, and get a clearer picture of your financial resources. Learn More and Apply 2026-2027 Student Loans Student Loan applications for the 2025-2026 to 2026-2027 academic year are available. Application processing is delayed pending announcement of final rules regarding changes to the Federal Student Loan Program brought on by the One Big Beautiful Bill. Please stay tuned for additional updates. Learn More About Student Loans Important Deadlines 2025-26 Financial Aid Deadlines The following is a list of financial aid related deadlines for the 2025-26 academic year. *Spring semester only Application Type Deadline Notes Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) Appeal February 13, 202

. *Spring semester only Application Type Deadline Notes Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) Appeal February 13, 2026* Professional Judgement Request May 1, 2026* Incomplete requests will not be considered for review. Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) May 8, 2026* The 2025-2026 FAFSA may be completed with your 2023 tax information. Student Loan Application April 20, 2026* Loan options may include federal loans and private loans. Tuition Assistance Program (TAP) Application June 30, 2026 * TAP is an income-based grant for eligible New York State residents. Excelsior Scholarship Appeal December 31, 2026 * Incomplete appeals will not be considered for review. Additional documentation must be submitted within two weeks after notification of an incomplete appeal. Tuition Assistance Program (TAP) Appeal February 13, 2026* TAP is an

an incomplete appeal. Tuition Assistance Program (TAP) Appeal February 13, 2026* TAP is an income-based grant for eligible New York State residents. Workshops & Events The Office of Financial Aid is currently offering FAFSA filing workshops and Financial Aid Wellness Orientation Sessions for all Hunter students. Learn More About Workshops & Events Financial Aid Verification For the 2026-27 academic year, you may now complete your financial aid verification process completely online using ProVerifier+. Learn More About Verification Financial Aid Calculators The following tools may be used to estimate financial aid and compare college costs: the CUNY Financial Aid Estimator shows potential aid for CUNY, the Federal Student Aid Estimator calculates federal aid eligibility, and the HESC TAP Calculator estimates NYS TAP awards for full-time study. CUNY Financial Aid Estimator Estimate potential financial aid for CUNY using your FAFSA SAI, helping you compare costs and plan for college. Federal Student

financial aid for CUNY using your FAFSA SAI, helping you compare costs and plan for college. Federal Student Aid Estimator Calculate federal aid eligibility based on your FAFSA SAI, providing an overview of grants and loans for college. New York State TAP Award Estimator Estimate New York State TAP funding for full-time study, helping NY residents plan state aid for college costs. Frequently Asked Questions For general questions about financial aid, FAFSA, TAP, loans, and/or summer funding, please review the Financial Aid FAQ. Visit Financial Aid FAQ Create a Support Ticket Student Support Portal Have a question about your financial aid? Create a support ticket through our Student Support Portal . Your inquiry will be automatically directed to the appropriate staff so that we can resolve your issue as quickly as possible. NetID username and password required. If you do not have a Hunter NetID, please contact us directly. Create a Support Ticket Arrow Up To Top
a Hunter NetID, please contact us directly. Create a Support Ticket Arrow Up To Top
